content:
3 min read
Just now

--

For years, setting up a local PHP and JavaScript web development environment has felt like picking the lesser of several evils. You could struggle with the configuration bloat of heavy setups like XAMPP, deal with the platform limits of Laragon (Windows only), or swallow the costly subscription tier hooks of modern premium alternatives.
Enter EnvKit (https://envkit.net/)—a free, lightweight, cross-platform local development suite built natively for Windows and macOS. It packages web servers, database engines, key-value stores, local mail catchers, and runtime variables directly into a clean, modern desktop interface. Even better? It comes with a built-in Model Context Protocol (MCP) server, allowing your AI coding assistants (like Cursor, Claude Code, or Windsurf) to fully interact with and manage your local infrastructure.
Here is exactly how to set up EnvKit from scratch on both Mac and Windows machines.
What’s Under the Hood?
EnvKit provides an impressive list of preconfigured services right out of the box, eliminating manual configurations:
Web Servers: Instantly toggle between Nginx or Apache, configured dynamically per site.
PHP Engine: Isolated runtime versions stretching from legacy PHP 7.4 up to the latest builds, with Xdebug on-demand.
Databases & Key-Value Stores: Oracle MySQL 8, MariaDB, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, and Redis
Developer Tooling & Admin UIs: Integrated phpMyAdmin, pgweb, mongo-express, Node.js dev servers, Composer, Git, and Mailpit for trapping local emails.
HTTPS: Trusted .test domains issued by a local certificate authority automatically.
Step-by-Step Installation
EnvKit supports Windows 10/11 and macOS 12 Monterey or later (specifically optimized for Apple Silicon).

• Setup on macOS (Apple Silicon only)
On a Mac, EnvKit provides a simple terminal command that pulls down the app, cleans the Gatekeeper quarantine, and shifts the utility cleanly into your environment:
Open your terminal app.
Run the official one-liner script:
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Env-Kit/envkit-releases/main/install.sh | bashNote on First Launch: Because the app is Developer-ID signed but navigating through final notarization pipelines, right-click the app icon in your Applications folder and select Open (or run xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine /Applications/EnvKit.app) to clear Apple's Gatekeeper guardrails.
2. Setup on Windows

• Navigate to the official release index or their website and download the latest EnvKit-Setup-*.exe binary.

• Launch the installer executable.

• If Windows SmartScreen displays a warning flag (typical for new self-signed executable binaries), click More Info — >Run Anyway.

• The installer automatically registers EnvKit’s local code-signing certificate into your Trusted Root folder, ensuring that future in-app updates happen seamlessly without repetitive popups.
First Launch & Migrating from Laragon
When you fire up EnvKit for the first time, a short onboarding wizard will pop up asking for your setup path:
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Activating the AI Integration (MCP Server)
One of EnvKit’s standout architectural wins is its native Model Context Protocol (MCP) layer. This allows an AI assistant running inside an IDE to inspect your system runtime, fix database hitches, or manage server parameters directly.
To enable this:

• Open EnvKit and click on Settings to AI.

• Toggle the Enable MCP Server switch.

• Click Set up AI clients. EnvKit will scan your system and automatically install the required operational skill parameters for Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code, or Zed.
Once initialized, you can open your AI chat window and issue plain-English instructions:
“Start Redis for me.” “Diagnose why MySQL won’t start, and then repair it.” “List my active local Laravel development sites.”
Summary: Why EnvKit is Worth the Switch
EnvKit manages to combine the platform simplicity of a desktop tray app with the advanced flexibility expected by modern web developers. By delivering a full menu of database systems, built-in HTTPS, hot-reloading Node.js setups, and AI terminal execution for free, it represents a massive step forward for both Windows and Mac development loops.
Are you planning to use EnvKit to replace a standard XAMPP/Laragon setup for local monolithic PHP apps, or are you utilizing its Node.js features to build headless Javascript frontends?
